Russell E-mini - Individual Market Analysis

Russell E-mini - Individual Market Analysis

CoT signals and diagnostics for Russell E-mini futures.

As of 2026-03-31

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec_Net_%OI of -4.5 and a Flow_4w of -2.8, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is a Regime shift, not a mean reversion. (1)
  • The signal is currently in a Balanced_Short_Bias regime, characterized by a persistent eight-week "Long_Reduction" flow cycle. (1)
  • Conviction Band: High; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Stable; Threshold Proximity: Moderate; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Flow Momentum: Multi-week long reductions in Flow_4w are interpreted as early recession signaling and a precursor to broader equity de-risking. (1)
  • Regime Bias: Falling Spec_zscore values imply credit stress or a transition to a risk-off environment. (1)
  • Russell E-Mini Signal: Tracks domestic growth expectations and small-cap balance sheet sensitivity to funding stress. (1)
  • Latest observation date: 2026-03-31.

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the sustained Long_Reduction capital flow (-8.6 strength at peak), indicating fading conviction among speculators. (1)
  • Positioning Tension has stabilized at low levels (0.81) following a sharp deleveraging from January peaks (3.78). (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: A reversal to positive 4-week capital flow (Long_Build) would invalidate the current downward bias.
  • The signal demonstrates high persistence in its current bearish trajectory since the February regime shift. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Continued price-path erosion as speculative support is withdrawn amid tightening domestic conditions.
  • Upside risk: Tactical relief rally triggered by short-covering if Spec_zscore reaches extreme negative levels (<-2.0).
  • Downside risk: Accelerated liquidation if credit spreads widen, exposing small-cap funding vulnerabilities.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks): The 4-week flow momentum dominates short-term price action.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: High: As a proxy for marginal risk tolerance, this signal provides leading context for broader US equity sentiment.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the US Growth & Business Cycle by gauging the health of credit-sensitive domestic firms. (2)
  • Implies a Liquidity withdrawal mechanism; as speculators reduce exposure, the market becomes more sensitive to funding shocks. (3)
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interacts with Credit Spreads and Bank Lending Standards to confirm recessionary transitions.

Regime Context:

  • The signal transitioned from a Balanced_Long_Bias to Balanced_Short_Bias in mid-February 2026. (1)
  • The current bearish regime is Persistent and Stabilizing at lower positioning levels.

Model Limitations:

  • Weak trend persistence and sensitivity to tactical, short-horizon flows can lead to frequent mean-reversion noise.
  • Weekly CoT reporting lag may mask intra-week volatility spikes.

Data & References:

  • RUSSELL E-MINI (CME) CoT Data through 2026-03-31. (1)
  • Influential datapoints: Spec_Net_%OI (-4.5) and Flow_4w (-2.8).
  • Public datasets: ICE BofA High Yield Spreads and St. Louis Fed Financial Stress Index for credit confirmation.
